title: Procession in the Sun: Boavista, Records, and a 12
title_candidates: Procession in the Sun: Boavista, Records, and a 12
theme: A four-day procession at Boavista in which the two big trophies were effectively decided by Wednesday lunchtime, while the record book quietly burned and Henry Meller's 17th-hole adventures provided the only real suspense.
opening_hook: Open on Boavista's 17th in Round 1 — Meller's 12 — then pull back to reveal that, while one man was carving an octuple into the par-4, Jon Baker was twelve holes into the lowest round in TEG history and the tournament was already, essentially, over.
foreshadow: Baker's R1 -9 (b04) — a TEG record on day one, planted as 'the day the chase ended before it began'
Mullin's R1 +17 gross (b03) — also a TEG record, foreshadowing a Jacket procession of his own
The 17th at Boavista — Meller's 12 in R1 (b02) sets up Mullin's 9 there in R2 and Neumann's 10 there in R4
Meller's spoon-bound drop on R1 H17 (b58) — he never really climbs out

competitions.name: The Trophy (Net vs Par)
competitions.how: Baker led from the first round to the last and never gave the lead up — the only lead change came on R1 hole 6 (David Mullin briefly tied), after which Baker's record-breaking -9 (b04) opened an eight-shot gap that only ever grew: 8 → 13 → 16 → 18. He added a -2 in R2 (b06, then 2nd-best ever), a -7 in R3 (b07), and was so far clear by Sunday that a level-par 0 closed it out at -18, winning by 18 shots. A procession, but a brilliant one.

competitions.name: The Green Jacket (Gross)
competitions.how: Mullin led the Jacket from R1 hole 4 to the final green and was never headed. His +17 in R1 (b03) was the best Gross round in TEG history; his +14 in R3 (b05) then broke that same record. Margins ballooned 2 → 5 → 9 → 12. A quintuple at the 17th in R2 (b17) and a quad at the 11th in R4 (b23) barely scratched the lead. Won at +72 by 12 from Stuart Neumann.

competitions.name: The Wooden Spoon
competitions.how: Meller's spoon was a wild ride rather than a steady slide. A 12 at the 17th in R1 (b02) shoved him to the bottom (b58); a stirring -2 in R2 (b08, b09) lifted him out and onto Neumann (b42); but R3 undid him for good — a triple at the 1st, then a quintuple-10 at the 3rd (b20) put him back at the foot (b35), and he stayed there despite a brave front-nine flurry. Finished at +16.

headline_candidates: Baker's -9, Mullin's +17, Meller's 12: the day the record book caught fire
Boavista Day One: two records broken, one par-4 turned into a par-12
rounds.chosen_headline: Baker's -9, Mullin's +17, Meller's 12: the day the record book caught fire
rounds.angle: Two TEG records (Baker net, Mullin gross) on the same afternoon effectively decide both top prizes, while Meller's 12 at the 17th sends him to the foot of the spoon.

headline_candidates: Meller's redemption, Mullin's blip, Baker's drift onwards
-2 for Meller — and a 9 at the 17th for Mullin
The day the chasers chased themselves
rounds.chosen_headline: Meller's -2 and Mullin's 9: the chase that wasn't
rounds.angle: Meller posts the 2nd-best round in TEG history (b08/b09) and briefly escapes the spoon; Mullin takes a 9 at the 17th but still extends the Jacket lead; Baker adds another sub-par round and pulls further clear.

headline_candidates: Mullin breaks his own record; Meller breaks himself
The 16-shot lead and the 10 at the 3rd
rounds.chosen_headline: Mullin breaks his own record; Meller breaks himself
rounds.angle: Mullin's +14 gross (b05) eclipses his own week-old TEG mark; Baker's -7 (b07) pushes the Trophy lead to 16; Meller's 10 at the 3rd (b20) hands the spoon back for good (b35); Williams and Neumann post notable rounds of their own.

headline_candidates: Baker home by 18, Mullin by 12 — and Neumann finds a 10
A coronation, a Jacket, and one last 17th-hole disaster
Boavista signs off in the only way it knows how
rounds.chosen_headline: Baker home by 18, Mullin by 12 — and Neumann finds a 10
rounds.angle: Both leaders cruise to record-margin wins; Neumann's 10 at the 17th (b15) is Boavista's parting shot; Meller seals the spoon at +16.

players.arc: Won the Trophy in the most emphatic style imaginable — record-breaking opening round, lead in every round, winning margin of 18; only a R3 quintuple at the 18th and a R3 quad at the 5th suggested he was mortal.
Twice broke the TEG record for lowest Gross round in the space of three days and led the Jacket wire-to-wire, surviving his own 9 at the 17th in R2.
The week's most volatile card: a 12 at the 17th in R1, the 2nd-best round in TEG history in R2, a 10 at the 3rd in R3, and the Wooden Spoon at the end of it.
Jacket runner-up on the strength of a record-equalling +18 gross in R3, undone slightly by his own 10 at the 17th in the final round.
Boavista's serial quadruple-bogey merchant — a R3 -4 (b14) buried among a remarkable collection of sevens, eights and nines.

cuts: b17 (Mullin's 9 at 17, R2) — referenced in passing only
b18, b22, b23, b24, b25, b26, b27, b28, b29, b30, b31, b32 — the long tail of quadruple bogeys; mention Williams's collection en bloc rather than itemise
b19, b36, b37, b41, b47, b48, b50 — hot stretches that don't change anything
b33, b34, b42 — earlier spoon shuffles superseded by b35
b38, b39, b40, b43, b44, b45, b46, b49 — minor narrative texture, cut for pace
venue_notes: Four rounds at Boavista Golf & Spa Resort, Lagos — TEG's 4th–7th rounds at this Howard Swan parkland and TEG's 2nd visit to the Algarve (2010). Lean on the Atlantic-view parkland feel, the two-sections quirk, and above all the 17th, which devoured Meller (12), Mullin (9) and Neumann (10) across the week — a recurring character in its own right.
